title: Keka HR API Vocabulary
description: Domain vocabulary and terminology for the Keka HR and payroll platform

terms.definition: A person employed by an organization and managed within Keka HRMS
A Keka customer organization identified by a unique subdomain
Human Resource Information System - the core employee data module
A formal employee request for time off from work
A category of leave (e.g., casual, sick, earned) defined by the organization
A time-stamped log of an employee's check-in and check-out events
A grouping of employees who share the same payroll processing schedule
An individual element of an employee's compensation (e.g., basic pay, HRA)
Professional Services Automation - module for managing billable projects and resources
Performance Management System - module for goals, appraisals, and feedback
Recruitment module for managing job openings and candidates
Background Verification - module for employee background check integrations
A formal request to fill a job position
Authorization protocol used for API authentication in Keka
OAuth 2.0 grant type used for machine-to-machine API access
Keka APIs return paginated results with page number, page size, and navigation links
API usage limits imposed per tenant to ensure platform stability
Event-driven HTTP callbacks triggered by actions in the Keka platform
A business expense submitted by an employee for reimbursement
A company-owned item assigned to an employee
Rules defining employee work shift timings and rotation patterns
Rules defining which days of the week are non-working for employees
A list of company-defined holidays for a specific period and location
An external platform where job openings are published for recruitment
An organizational unit grouping employees by function
A physical or virtual work site associated with employees
A flexible grouping of employees used for policies and reporting
The required period of notice before an employee leaves the organization
The categorized reason for an employee leaving the organization
